decision: osspulse schedule generates an OS crontab line for osspulse run; print-only by default
reasoning: PROJECT_SPEC §8 fixes OS cron as the primary scheduler and forbids a background service; print-only default avoids implicitly mutating the operator's environment

decision: osspulse stays single-shot; timing delegated entirely to OS cron or CI cron
reasoning: PROJECT_SPEC §8 'Không cần service chạy nền phức tạp' + §7 lean principles

decision: Generated cron/CI artifacts must never inline a secret; reference env/.env or repo secrets store
reasoning: PROJECT_SPEC §8 security notes + github-collector-2 ADR-004; committing a workflow with an inlined token would leak it into git
rejected: inline token into crontab line for convenience

decision: --install/--uninstall confined to a marker-delimited managed block; preserve everything else
reasoning: Editing the operator's crontab risks clobbering unrelated jobs; a managed block makes the operation idempotent and reversible

decision: osspulse run acquires a single-instance lock under state_path.parent before the pipeline
reasoning: A fast cron cadence could fire a second run while the first is mid-flight, racing the JSON state file (load->mark_seen->save) and losing updates
rejected: no lock (rely on runs being short)
DB-level lock (no DB in V1/V2 JSON store)

decision: An overlapping run (lock held) logs WARN and exits 0 — benign skip
reasoning: Exiting non-zero would make cron email a spurious failure every overlap; a benign skip keeps cron mail meaningful

decision: Use an OS advisory lock (fcntl.flock) auto-released by the kernel on process death
reasoning: Avoids stale-lock deadlock after kill -9 without needing pidfile staleness heuristics; architect to confirm mechanism at S3

decision: Lock path + config/binary paths derived in the pipeline (state_path.parent), not new Config fields
reasoning: Follows scheduler-cli-7 ADR-002 / github-collector-2 ADR-001 discipline of not adding Config fields for adapter tunables

decision: Default cadence (no flag) is daily 08:00 local time (0 8 * * *)
reasoning: A morning digest is the natural default for a 'what happened' summary; operator can override with --cron/--preset

decision: Generated crontab invocation uses absolute binary + config paths
reasoning: cron runs with a minimal cwd/PATH; a relative path would silently break the scheduled run
rejected: relative paths assuming a working directory

decision: Generated binary path resolved via shutil.which('osspulse') with absolute sys.argv[0] fallback; config path made absolute; ASSUMED->CONFIRMED
reasoning: cron minimal PATH/cwd makes relative paths unsafe; an absolute path is deterministic and needs no cron-PATH probe

decision: NEW AC: --install writes the resolved absolute-path line and does NOT verify the cron daemon PATH; resolves the S1 [UNCLEAR]
reasoning: emitting an absolute path makes cron-PATH verification unnecessary and avoids probing an environment cron will not reproduce
rejected: shell out to verify binary on cron PATH

decision: Default cadence (no flag) confirmed daily 08:00 local = 0 8 * * *; ASSUMED->CONFIRMED
reasoning: morning-digest default matches the 'what happened' use case; operator overrides with --cron/--preset

decision: crontab command unavailable -> Error: <message> stderr, no traceback, exit 1; ASSUMED->CONFIRMED
reasoning: matches the CLI error contract (Error: prefix, exit 1 for handled fatal, no stacktrace)

decision: --output unwritable path -> Error: exit 1, no partial file; ASSUMED->CONFIRMED
reasoning: fail-fast, no-partial-write matches BR-V2-002-003 and the CLI error contract

decision: Generated workflow documents its cron as UTC via a YAML comment; ASSUMED->CONFIRMED
reasoning: GitHub Actions schedule.cron is UTC while OS cron is local; documenting prevents silent off-by-hours (memory: cron TZ lesson)

decision: No ANSI color when stdout is not a TTY; ASSUMED->CONFIRMED
reasoning: ANSI codes leaking into cron mail/log files are noise; TTY-detection at the CLI boundary keeps unattended output clean

decision: Overlap benign skip = WARN + exit 0, NOT a distinct non-zero code; ASSUMED->CONFIRMED (contentious point resolved)
reasoning: a non-zero code would make cron email a spurious failure on every overlap; benign skip keeps cron mail meaningful. Distinct-code alternative explicitly rejected per _handoff §2

decision: Lock mechanism pinned to fcntl.flock advisory lock auto-released by kernel on process death; ASSUMED->CONFIRMED
reasoning: kernel auto-release avoids stale-lock deadlock after kill -9 with no pidfile staleness heuristic; architect confirms platform semantics + derive-not-Config-field at S3

decision: New schedule/ package (cron.py, crontab.py, workflow.py, secrets.py, errors.py) + top-level lock.py; lock acquired at CLI boundary around run_pipeline
reasoning: Isolates 3 concerns + secret guard for independent mocking; mirrors github/ state/ delivery/ layout; lock is a run-time CLI-boundary concern (AC-V2-002-021) orthogonal to schedule, keeps benign-skip in the CLI error boundary; pipeline.py body untouched

decision: resolve_binary(): shutil.which('osspulse') -> fallback os.path.abspath(sys.argv[0]); config path via Path(config).resolve(); no cron-PATH verification
reasoning: which finds installed console-script (pipx/pip) as absolute path; sys.argv[0] abspath covers python -m/local; absolute path makes cron minimal-PATH probing pointless (AC-V2-002-024)

decision: In-repo dependency-free validate_cron_expr (5-field, per-field numeric-range check); presets bypass; mutual-exclusion at CLI; validate before any I/O
reasoning: lean-stack principle avoids a new runtime dep for a small need; rejects 99 * * * * and malformed specs deterministically; resolve->validate->write order guarantees no partial side effect (BR-V2-002-003)

decision: single_instance_lock context manager: fcntl.flock(fd, LOCK_EX|LOCK_NB) on state_path.parent/osspulse.lock (0o600); BlockingIOError->LockHeldError; LOCK_UN+close in finally; path derived not Config field
reasoning: kernel auto-releases advisory lock on process death incl kill -9 -> no stale-lock heuristic (AC-V2-002-023); LOCK_NB makes held lock raise immediately -> benign skip never blocks (AC-V2-002-022); derive from state_path.parent upholds scheduler-cli-7 ADR-002

decision: Two error classes: ScheduleError (fatal->exit 1) in schedule/errors.py; LockHeldError (benign->exit 0) in lock.py; CLI matches LockHeldError FIRST
reasoning: overlap must exit 0 (AC-V2-002-022) so it cannot be a plain exit-1 fatal; separate class + first-matched arm keeps the exit-1 error contract intact; order-dependent arms mirror pipeline ADR-003
rejected: one ScheduleError with benign boolean flag

decision: Shared collect_secret_values(env)+assert_no_secret(text,values) in schedule/secrets.py called by BOTH cron.generate_line and workflow.generate_workflow before returning/writing
reasoning: RISK-001 HIGH single choke-point; both surfaces (crontab line + Actions YAML, the hardest) provably covered by one test; runtime backstop = defense-in-depth atop by-construction env/secrets refs (AC-V2-002-005/-015)

decision: Pure upsert_block/remove_block with pinned markers '# >>> osspulse >>>'/'# <<< osspulse <<<'; replace inclusive region or append (leading \n only when appending to non-empty no-trailing-newline); remove strips exact region; round-trip byte-identical
reasoning: RISK-002 idempotent replace + byte-for-byte preservation (AC-V2-002-010/-011); pure string funcs fully unit-testable without a real crontab; precise newline symmetry guarantees remove(upsert(x))==x

decision: CrontabClient wrapping subprocess (read()=crontab -l normalized to '' when empty; write()=crontab - via stdin); raise ScheduleError when crontab binary absent
reasoning: single mockable OS seam so tests never touch the real user crontab; pure block funcs do the logic; matches ports/adapters style; normalizes crontab -l exit-1-when-empty to '' (AC-V2-002-013)
rejected: inline subprocess in command handler (not mockable)

decision: No openapi.yaml — CLI tool, no HTTP surface; CLI contract (flags+exit codes) is the interface
reasoning: R5 requires openapi only if the change has an API; adds a CLI subcommand only; cites state-store-3 ADR-004 / github-collector-2 ADR-007 / scheduler-cli-7 precedent (all CLI-only skipped openapi)
rejected: author openapi.yaml anyway (fabricates non-existent HTTP contract)

decision: TTY/color + no-prompt hardening at CLI boundary: sys.stdout.isatty() no-color guard; reaffirm scheduler-cli-7 cron-mail-friendly logging + exit contract via tests, not new logging
reasoning: handoff §3 places TTY/color detection at CLI boundary not pipeline stages; AC-V2-002-018..020 largely verify existing cron-safe behavior; keeps pipeline pure

decision: Converted Preset(str, Enum) to Preset(StrEnum) (ruff UP042 auto-fix)
reasoning: StrEnum is canonical Python 3.11+; semantically equivalent; required to pass ruff lint gate

decision: Removed CliRunner(mix_stderr=False) — Typer 0.26.7 does not support this kwarg
reasoning: Typer's CliRunner signature only accepts charset and env; result.stderr works as separate attribute; no semantic change to tests

decision: Created test_cli_run_cronsafe.py (task 6.3) — file was absent from workspace
reasoning: 20 tests covering AC-V2-002-018..023; uses real fcntl for lock tests (ADR-004), CliRunner for CLI contract tests, caplog for WARN assertion; all pass

decision: Added README §Scheduling section covering OS cron primary/Actions optional/UTC-local TZ divergence/benign-skip/python-m gotcha
reasoning: Task 7.1 requirement; addresses all 5 documentation points from the architect's watch items

decision: Misleading test name: test_upsert_block_no_double_newline_when_existing_ends_with_newline asserts double-\n IS present (opposite of name)
reasoning: S4 test authoring — test name contradicts assertion. Code behavior is correct per ADR-007 round-trip guarantee. SPEC-UNCLEAR: test name vs actual ADR intent.

decision: _is_tty guard in cli.run is documentation-only (F841 unused); no-color enforcement not actively wired to any output path
reasoning: S4 implementation — ADR-010 framed this as reaffirmation via tests. Variable is computed but never passed to Typer/logging output paths. Current output is provably ANSI-free (test passes). Risk is future regression if colored output is added. Severity Low.

decision: BrokenPipeError handler (cli.py:85-87) has 0% coverage — untestable via CliRunner; requires live pipe smoke test
reasoning: S4 inherent limitation — CliRunner uses BytesIO (no fileno), SIGPIPE cannot be triggered. Documented in memory/qa/scheduler-cli-7.md from prior change. EDGE-CASE carry-over. Added to S6 operator checklist.

decision: GO — 0 Critical/High bugs, 24/24 ACs verified, 96.22% coverage, dependency audit clean, ruff clean
reasoning: All gate criteria met. 3 Low bugs are non-blocking: B-001 SPEC-UNCLEAR test name, B-002 acknowledged tech debt per ADR-010, B-003 known untestable EDGE-CASE. Security mitigations (RISK-001/002/003/004) independently verified via code review and real-fd kernel tests.
rejected: NO-GO on B-002 — ADR-010 explicitly accepts this as reaffirmation design

decision: Renamed test_upsert_block_no_double_newline_when_existing_ends_with_newline → test_upsert_block_adds_newline_separator_when_existing_ends_with_newline
reasoning: Old name said 'no double newline' but the assertion verified that double-\n IS present (the round-trip sentinel per ADR-007). Rename makes the name match the actual ADR-007 contract being verified.
rejected: add comment explaining the contradiction (obscures intent)

decision: Wired _is_tty: when not a TTY, set os.environ['NO_COLOR']='1' before pipeline invocation; removed F841 noqa suppression
reasoning: ADR-010 requires no-color enforcement when non-TTY. Previously the variable was computed but never acted upon. Setting NO_COLOR before pipeline ensures any future color-aware output (Typer, logging) respects the guard. Tests added: test_run_sets_no_color_env_when_not_tty verifies env var is set; test_run_no_color_not_set_when_tty verifies TTY path.

decision: Extracted _handle_broken_pipe() helper with hasattr(sys.stdout,'fileno') guard + io.UnsupportedOperation catch; BrokenPipeError handler in run() calls _handle_broken_pipe()
reasoning: os.dup2(devnull, sys.stdout.fileno()) was untestable via CliRunner (BytesIO has no fileno). Extracting to a helper with the guard makes both branches (real fd and no-fd) independently testable. Added 4 tests: with real fd, without fileno (hasattr guard), UnsupportedOperation catch, and behavioral exit-0 test via CliRunner.
rejected: inline try/except in run() (still 0% coverage on dup2 branch)

decision: QA re-verify PASS — 429 passed (+6 new tests), 96.47% coverage (+0.25 pp), all 3 bugs confirmed RESOLVED
reasoning: B-001: test renamed to test_upsert_block_adds_newline_separator_when_existing_ends_with_newline — old name gone, passes. B-002: _is_tty now wired to os.environ['NO_COLOR']='1' when non-TTY; two new tests cover both TTY/non-TTY branches. B-003: _handle_broken_pipe() extracted with hasattr+UnsupportedOperation guards; 4 new tests cover all branches (real fd, no fileno, UnsupportedOperation, behavioral exit-0). Ruff still clean. Coverage improved from 96.22% to 96.47% — previously uncovered cli.py:85-87 (BrokenPipeError handler body) now covered. Remaining uncovered: cli.py:70 (def line), 277-280 (finally unlink cleanup) — pre-existing, acceptable. GO confirmed.
